title: Reproductive Freedom as Democratic Architecture
advocacy_window: Active — 2026 ballot measures in MO/NV/VA/ID/NE; WHPA before Congress; mifepristone SCOTUS trajectory
justification: Largest organized constituency of any Phase 2 candidate (Reproductive Freedom for All/NARAL/PPAF). Democratic-exclusion framing is genuinely new — existing advocacy covers rights claim but not how restrictions function as civic exclusion mechanisms. 7-of-7 post-Dobbs ballot measures protected abortion access; movement has demonstrated direct-democracy organizing capacity. Phase 1 integration: Domain 33 (ballot initiative suppression), Domain 1 (NVRA nexus), Domain 31 (Medicaid-reproductive care). Adoption path: national-to-local distribution through PPAF 51 affiliates.

title: Labor Rights NLRB and Worker Power as Democratic Infrastructure
advocacy_window: Active — NLRB constitutional challenge (SpaceX/Amazon/Trader Joe's/Starbucks) likely at SCOTUS in 12-18 months; PRO Act positioning for 2027 Congress
justification: AFL-CIO (12.5M members) and SEIU (2M members) have research departments and member education infrastructure that would integrate domain materials into existing campaigns. The NLRB constitutional attack framing — a democracy crisis not a labor dispute — is the analytical hook that makes this adoption likely beyond existing labor advocacy. Phase 1 integration: Domain 6 (Humphrey's Executor collapse), Domain 38 (parallel corporate capture pattern), Domain 29 (DOJ weaponization against organizers). Adoption path: AFL-CIO research department → member education curriculum.

title: Housing Instability Eviction and Democratic Exclusion
advocacy_window: Medium — OBBBA Medicaid-housing nexus creates Aug-Sep 2026 window as work requirement comment period opens; tenant organizing surge in NY/CA
justification: Eviction Lab (Slee and Desmond 2023) provides robust empirical foundation: eviction reduces voter turnout as causal mechanism not mere correlation. Housing organizing constituency (tenant unions/homeless coalitions/housing court advocates) is genuinely grassroots — not currently reached by any Phase 1 target. Gap-filling impact is very high: no existing domain addresses housing through democratic-exclusion lens. Phase 1 integration: Domain 31 (Medicaid-housing nexus), Domain 1 (eviction-turnout evidence), Domain 22 (racial disparity in eviction rates). Adoption path: NLIHC → Housing Justice for All → tenant union networks.

title: Campaign Finance Dark Money and Corporate Capture of Democratic Institutions
advocacy_window: Active — DISCLOSE Act (47 Senate Ds/139 House Ds) live legislation; 4 state dark money ballot measures in 2026; Arizona Prop 211 challenge
justification: Meta-domain that ties together corporate capture vectors documented throughout Phase 1: crypto-PAC capture (D38) pharma-lobbying (D42) anti-union corporate campaigns (D45) dark money attacking ballot initiatives (D33). Reform organizations (Common Cause/Campaign Legal Center/Issue One) have existing distribution infrastructure that would amplify to their networks. Phase 1 integration: Domain 38 (financial sector analog) Domain 33 (dark money funding ballot initiative suppression) Domain 42 (pharmaceutical lobbying) Domain 35 (SCOTUS campaign finance OT2026). Adoption path: Common Cause state chapters → cross-partisan coalition.

title: Environmental Justice and Community Democratic Participation in Environmental Decision-Making
advocacy_window: Ongoing — NEPA repeal (Jan 8 2026) closure of all 10 EPA EJ offices; coalition of 18 state AGs litigation active
justification: NEPA repeal eliminated the primary mechanism through which low-income communities and communities of color could contest decisions imposing pollution burdens. EPA EJ office closure ($400M grants cancelled) directly attacks institutional infrastructure. EJ organizations (Earthjustice/NRDC/Indigenous Environmental Network) are legally sophisticated actors with litigation and advocacy capacity. Phase 1 integration: Domain 15 (climate policy) Domain 6 (litigation challenges to regulatory rollbacks) Domain 26 (government accountability). Adoption path: Earthjustice → NRDC EJ program → state-level EJ coalitions.

title: Indigenous Sovereignty Treaty Rights and Tribal Democratic Participation
advocacy_window: Ongoing — FY2027 appropriations battle over tribal cuts; BIA reorganization administrative proceedings; UNDRIP implementation advocacy
justification: Revocation of EO 14112 on Tribal Sovereignty (March 2025) elimination of $1.5B in clean energy grants to tribal communities BIA office closures and IHS cuts represent most direct attacks on tribal self-determination since termination era. Framework gap is severe — 574 federally recognized sovereign governments receive no standalone domain analysis. NCAI and NARF are institutionally sophisticated distribution nodes. Phase 1 integration: Domain 22 (reparations/racial justice) Domain 15 (environment — treaty rights) Domain 9 (federalism — nation-to-nation) Domain 40 (constitutional architecture). Adoption path: NCAI member tribes → tribal colleges → indigenous organizing networks.

title: Disability Rights Civic Access and Democratic Participation of Disabled Americans
advocacy_window: Ongoing — Section 504 regulatory rollback litigation; ACL cuts reducing independent living center funding; ADA enforcement rollback
justification: Domain 41b covers disability through benefits-access lens; civic access dimension (57% of disabled people experience barriers to civic engagement; 11% report difficulty voting) requires distinct treatment. ACL cuts reducing independent living center funding directly remove the organizations that interface between disabled individuals and civic institutions. Phase 1 integration: Domain 41b (benefits access — companion document) Domain 1 (voting access barriers) Domain 26 (government accountability — ADA enforcement). Adoption path: 400 independent living centers nationwide → state protection and advocacy organizations → NCIL.

title: Felony Disenfranchisement and Re-Enfranchisement as Democratic Repair
advocacy_window: Active — state campaigns in MA/WA/NH; Prison Policy Initiative 2026 winnable reforms tracker; active legislative sessions in 8-10 states
justification: 4 million Americans disenfranchised; 1 in 22 Black adults. Domain 1 mentions but does not analyze as standalone democratic design failure. Domain 42 covers drug-enforcement nexus but not the broader re-enfranchisement reform architecture. State-by-state campaign map (which legislatures have votes for reform; which have ballot initiative pathways) is the distinctive contribution. Phase 1 integration: Domain 1 (electoral reform foundation) Domain 42 (drug policy — disenfranchisement nexus) Domain 22 (racial justice). Adoption path: Sentencing Project → Prison Policy Initiative → state-level re-enfranchisement coalitions.

title: Civil Society Suppression and the Nonprofit Independence Crisis
advocacy_window: Active — Section 112209 OBBBA enacted law; Florida HB 1471 (July 1 2026 effective); IRS/FBI task force operational; SPLC indictment creates live case study
justification: Section 112209 grants Treasury Secretary unilateral authority to designate any nonprofit as 'terrorist supporting organization' — enacted law as of 2026. Florida HB 1471 effective July 1 2026 extends to state level. $163B proposed cut to non-defense programs including complete elimination of CDBG and CSBG. 33% of nonprofits report funding disruption. This is the structural demobilization of civil society — the mechanism that consolidated autocracies use to prevent opposition organizing. Phase 1 integration: Domain 29 (prosecutorial weaponization — IRS parallel) Domain 3 (democratic participation delivery mechanism) Domain 7 (First Amendment chilling effects) Domain 8 (nonprofit investigative journalism). Adoption path: National Council of Nonprofits → ICNL → Leadership Conference.

title: Mutual Aid Networks Tax Law and the Legal Infrastructure of Democratic Solidarity
advocacy_window: Ongoing — IRS guidance gap creates tax liability for mutual aid participants; Time (March 2026) documented legal gray zone; narrow statutory fix achievable in current Congress
justification: Mutual aid networks are the most decentralized and resilient form of democratic organization — demonstrated during COVID-19 and during 2025-2026 funding freeze. The legal gray zone (monetary transfers fall outside clear IRS guidance) is a structural barrier to democratic self-organization. The policy ask is narrow and technically achievable: IRS guidance or statutory exclusion for communal mutual aid transfers. Phase 1 integration: Domain 7 (freedom of association) Domain 26 (IRS as administrative tool) Domain 5 (tax law democratic effects). Adoption path: mutual aid network coordinators (Signal groups/Slack communities) → movement-specific digital channels → direct-to-organizer distribution.

title: Digital Organizing Secure Communications and Technical Infrastructure of Democratic Resistance
advocacy_window: Ongoing — Penlink subscription expansion; LAPD drone expansion; facial recognition class action; Q1-Q2 2026 surveillance threat vectors documented
justification: Cybersecurity hardening trilogy covers individual operational security. This candidate covers the organizational dimension: how civil society organizations secure communications protect organizing databases and maintain capacity under sustained surveillance pressure. Distinct audience: organizing directors not individual activists. Phase 1 integration: Domain 21 (data privacy and surveillance) Domain 36 (AI governance — algorithmic content moderation) Domain 7 (First Amendment protections for organizing communications). Adoption path: movement-specific Slack/Signal channels → organizing director network → Labor Notes / Movement for Black Lives infrastructure.

title: Rural Democracy and Agricultural Community Civic Participation
advocacy_window: Active — farm bill cuts; tariff retaliation affecting agricultural exports; rural hospital closures; National Farmers Union organizing
justification: Framework has documented gap in rural organizing constituencies. Rural communities are affected by Domain 23 (tariffs) Domain 31 (rural hospital closures) Domain 15 (environmental rollbacks) but none of these domains are framed for rural audiences or distributed through rural organizing channels. Agricultural community organized civic infrastructure (National Farmers Union/rural co-ops) is not currently reached by Phase 1 or Phase 2 targets. Phase 1 integration: Domain 23 (tariff retaliation) Domain 31 (rural hospital closures) Domain 9 (federalism) Domain 15 (WOTUS). Adoption path: National Farmers Union → rural co-op networks → rural hospital coalition.

title: Voting Rights Restoration 50-State Playbook for the 2026-2028 Window
advocacy_window: Active 2026-2028 — specific state legislative sessions identified; ballot initiative architecture in 26 states allows citizen-initiated constitutional amendments
justification: Operationally focused companion to Domain 1 (Electoral Reform) and proposed Domain 51 (Felony Disenfranchisement). Domain 1 documents the crisis; this domain documents the remedial architecture — specific state-by-state analysis of which chambers have votes for re-enfranchisement legislation which courts could force registration access which states have ballot initiative pathways. Phase 1 integration: Domain 1 (primary foundation) Domain 33 (preemption/ballot initiative suppression context) Domain 37 (electoral integrity context). Adoption path: Democracy Docket → state-level voting rights coalitions → civic engagement organizations.

title: Criminal Justice Reform and Democratic Participation of Justice-Involved Communities
advocacy_window: Ongoing — Sentencing Project/Prison Policy Initiative/Justice Lab at Columbia active research pipeline; state legislative sessions in multiple states
justification: Domain 14 (Criminal Justice and Policing) is a proposal-body entry with no standalone research file. This candidate focuses specifically on democratic participation dimension — civic exclusion effects beyond disenfranchisement including jury exclusion public employment restrictions housing discrimination and debt burden following criminal records. Phase 1 integration: Domain 14 (criminal justice foundation) Domain 22 (racial disparity) Domain 42 (drug enforcement as primary driver of mass incarceration) Domain 1 (felony disenfranchisement nexus). Adoption path: Sentencing Project → Prison Policy Initiative → Justice Lab → local re-entry organizations.

title: Immigrant Communities Civic Participation and Surveillance Chilling Effect on Democratic Organization
advocacy_window: Ongoing — mass deportation enforcement active; LA November 2026 noncitizen voting ballot measure; birthright citizenship litigation at SCOTUS OT2026-27
justification: Domain 16 (Immigration and Citizenship) covers immigration enforcement and legal rights. This candidate covers the civic participation chilling effect: how mass deportation threats community surveillance and ICE enforcement actions suppress civic participation of both undocumented immigrants and their US citizen family members and neighbors. Research documents reduced participation in public schools healthcare and civic institutions. Phase 1 integration: Domain 16 (immigration foundation) Domain 21 (data privacy — ICE data tools) Domain 36 (AI governance — ImmigrationOS) Domain 7 (First Amendment chilling effects). Adoption path: RAICES → Make the Road → ACLU Immigrants Rights Project → local immigrant services coalitions.
